title: The Recurrent Nova T Pyx: Distance and Remnant Geometry from Light Echoes
notes: Transient reflection nebula around T Pyx; clumpy ring radius about 5 arcsec; distance 4.8 ± 0.5 kpc; ring embedded in quasi-spherical shell.

title: Flash of Light from Erupting Star Illuminates Debris Disk
notes: Public Hubble page with image dates Sept. 16, Nov. 16, Dec. 10 2011; notes disk about a light-year across.

title: Flash of light from erupting star T Pyxidis illuminates debris disc
notes: ESA/Hubble page for the same image set; describes sequential illumination of debris disk and 3-D mapping.

title: The 2011 Outburst of Recurrent Nova T Pyx: Radio Observations Reveal the Ejecta Mass and Hint at Complex Mass Loss
notes: Radio emission implies ejecta mass of 1-30 × 10^-5 solar masses, depending on filling factor and geometry.

title: HST Images Flash Ionization of Old Ejecta by the 2011 Eruption of Recurrent Nova T Pyxidis
notes: Seven HST Hα epochs from 4 to 383 days after eruption; no effect until at least day 55; ionization seen by day 132; recombination by day 183; additional structures lit between days 132 and 183; little hydrogen by day 282 and again 101 days later.

title: The Nova Shell and Evolution of the Recurrent Nova T Pyxidis
notes: Knots expand at roughly 500-715 km/s; 1866 ejecta total mass about 10^-4.5 solar masses.

title: Infrared observations of the recurrent nova T Pyxidis
notes: Eruption heated dust in pre-existing nebulosity; dust most likely interstellar swept up by T Pyx rather than freshly produced in eruptions.

title: The 2011 outburst of the recurrent nova T Pyxidis. Evidence for a face-on bipolar ejection
notes: Interferometry found slow expansion velocities ≤300 km/s before day 20; later data from day 28 onward favored bipolar morphology with faster expansion.

title: The physical properties of T Pyx as measured by MUSE
use_case: 3D geometry / nebular parallax / blob volume method
notes: MUSE analysis describes a two-component remnant (tilted ring plus fast bipolar ejecta) and discusses using measured blob size and distance to estimate volume and an upper limit on bipolar ejecta mass.

title: The 2011 Eruption of the Recurrent Nova T Pyxidis; the Discovery, the Pre-eruption Rise, the Pre-eruption Orbital Period, and the Reason for the Long Delay
notes: Discovery/eruption reference epoch JD 2455665.7931 = UT 2011 April 14.29.
